content: TURN relay for WebRTC data channels: TURN is media-agnostic at the relay layer — it relays UDP packets regardless of whether they carry audio, video, or data channel DTLS/SCTP traffic. coturn handles this transparently. Configuration: realm, user credentials, listening port (3478 UDP/TCP), relay port range (49152-65535). ICE candidate types: host (LAN IP), srflx (STUN-mapped public IP), relay (TURN-allocated relay IP). Browser RTCPeerConnection iceServers config accepts both stun: and turn: URLs. TURN is only used when host and srflx candidates fail. For IMU data channels, relay through TURN adds 1-2 extra network hops (~10-40ms penalty) but ensures connectivity in restrictive NAT environments. Relay traffic for small IMU packets is cheap — no per-MB billing concern unlike video TURN costs.
